column42: 12.1 Goals and objectives met: Ensuring that goals and objectives of the enterprise are met
column43: 12.2 Governance: How an organization oversees, monitors, and guides its Agile projects
column44: 12.3 Benefits realisation: The practice of ensuring that benefits are derived from outputs and outcomes
column45: 12.4 Market impact: Refers to imbedded market cost that buyers and investors may gain when buying or selling a product
column46: 12.5 Industry impact: It is away in which a specific industry impacts the society and other economically industries economically by improving their performance, efficiency and their innovation in their products and services
column47: 12.6 Competitive impact: Measures the effect of competition in organisations
column48: 12.Prefer not say Investor impact: Refers to the power of the investments made into organisations with the intention to financial return to the investors
column49: 12.8 Regulator impact: These are both negative and positive effects of regulators’ regulations towards organisations
column50: 12.9 Making the best of the portfolio’s financial benefits
column51: 12.10 The relationship between the organisation’s strategies with the portfolio
column52: 12.11 Balance of the products with organisations’ strength
column54: 13.1 Ownership benefits realised: The process of ensuring benefits are identified, defined, linked to strategic outcomes, delivered, and fully realised.
column55: 13.2 Alignment with programme benefits achieved : A dynamic state in which an organization's projects and initiatives are structured in a way that they contribute to a program's overall strategic direction and ultimately, desired business results
column56: 13.3 Compliance with standards met: Compliance means conforming to a rule, such as a specification, policy, standard or law
column57: 13.4 Strategy alignment: A process that ensures an organization's structure, use of resources support its strategy
column58: 13.5 Stakeholder engagement: The process by which an organization involves people who maybe affected by the decisions it makes or can influence the implementation of its decisions
column59: 14.1 Specifications met: Referring to when the customer specifications being met on the developed product
column60: 14.2 Customer acceptance: The acceptance of the product by the customer
column61: 14.3 Customer satisfaction: Customer satisfaction as a result of the delivered product
column62: 14.4 Schedule, budget and scope met : Product meeting delivered as per schedule, budget and scope
column63: 14.5 Product/system used: Final delivered product being used
column64: 14.Prefer not say Delivery of the product/service: Final product or service being delivered
